parts.text: The orphanage is high in the Carolina mountains. I was there in the autumn. I wanted quiet, isolation, to do some troublesome writing.

questions.question: What season was it when the narrator was at the orphanage?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states, 'I was there in the autumn.'

parts.text: I wanted mountain air to blow out the malaria from too long a time in the subtropics. I was homesick too, for the flaming of maples in October.

questions.question: The narrator wanted the mountain air to blow out the ___.
questions.explanation: She mentions wanting to clear out the malaria after spending too much time in the subtropics.

parts.text: I found them all living in a cabin that belonged to the orphanage, half a mile beyond the orphanage farm.

questions.question: Where was the cabin located in relation to the farm?
questions.explanation: The cabin was situated half a mile past the orphanage farm.

parts.text: When I took the cabin, I asked for a boy or man to come and chop wood for the fireplace.

questions.question: The narrator needed someone to ___ wood.
questions.explanation: She requested help specifically to chop wood for her fireplace.

parts.text: I looked up from my typewriter one late afternoon, a little startled. A boy stood at the door and my pointer dog, my companion, was at his side and had not barked to warn me.
The boy was probably twelve years old, but undersized. He wore overalls and a torn shirt, and was barefooted.

questions.question: What was unusual about the boy's appearance given the setting?
questions.explanation: The description highlights that he was barefooted despite being in the mountains.

parts.text: He said, 'I can chop some wood today.' 'You? But you're small.' 'Size don't matter, chopping wood,' he said.

questions.question: The boy argued that ___ does not matter when chopping wood.
questions.explanation: The boy directly states, 'Size don't matter, chopping wood.'

parts.text: 'Some of the big boys don't chop good. I've been chopping wood at the orphanage a long time.'

questions.question: Where did the boy gain his experience?
questions.explanation: He mentions he has been chopping wood at the orphanage for a long time.

parts.text: I went back to work, closing the door. He began to chop. The blows were rhythmic and steady, and shortly I had forgotten him.

questions.question: The narrator described the sound of the chopping as ___ and steady.
questions.explanation: The text describes the blows as 'rhythmic and steady'.

parts.text: I heard the boy's steps on the cabin stoop. We went together back of the cabin. An astonishing amount of solid wood had been cut. 'But you've done as much as a man,' I said.
